hero.headline: Education is Freedom
hero.subheadline: Since 2009, HCW has worked for educational equality in Central Africa. Every donation opens a door for a child.

campaign.title: Campaign — Education is Freedom
campaign.subtitle: Support 200+ vulnerable students in the Central African Republic
campaign.tier1_desc: Provides supplies + textbooks for one child for a year
campaign.tier2_desc: Covers meals for one student for a month in Bangui
campaign.tier3_desc: Finances a full term of schooling for one student
campaign.quote: « Every donation transforms a life in the Central African Republic »
campaign.photo_alt: Children benefiting from HCW programmes in the Central African Republic

volunteer.title: Become a Volunteer
volunteer.subtitle: Your skills can change a life. Join the HCW team.
volunteer.hero_subtitle: Join our network of committed volunteers and help bring education to Central Africa — from anywhere in the world or on the ground.
volunteer.benefit1_desc: Contribute from anywhere in the world, on your own schedule.
volunteer.benefit2_desc: See the direct effect of your commitment on thousands of students.
volunteer.benefit3_desc: Join an international community committed to education in Africa.
volunteer.role_communication_desc: Manage our social media, write content and raise awareness of our causes.
volunteer.role_education_desc: Develop educational content and support our literacy programmes.
volunteer.role_it_desc: Contribute to the eNdara platform and our online learning tools.
volunteer.role_fundraising_desc: Help us organise events and find new donors.

volunteer.whatsapp_note: Join our volunteer group and stay informed.

partners.page_description: Meet HCW's partners: MEYNG, WEIRAM. Together we drive education and solidarity in Central Africa.

partners.become_subtitle: Join our network of organisations committed to education in Central Africa.
partners.partner_meyng_desc: Technology partner — eNdara platform development, a virtual learning space enabling students in CAR to access digital educational resources.
partners.partner_weiram_desc: Community partner — community mobilisation in CAR, local grounding and strengthening of civil society to maximise the impact of our programmes.
partners.offer_visibility: Visibility on our website and social media channels
partners.offer_events: Invitation to HCW events (eNdara Challenge, annual gala)

partners.type_inkind_desc: Provision of materials, equipment or services valued as financial equivalent.
partners.type_technical_desc: Expertise, training and skills transfer benefiting our programme participants.
partners.type_institutional_desc: Collaboration with public organisations, NGOs or academic institutions.
partners.cta_title: Let's build a better future together
partners.cta_subtitle: Every partnership amplifies the impact of our programmes for children in CAR.

impactPage.page_description: 800+ laureates, 9,000+ students reached since 2009. Discover HCW's impact in Central Africa.
impactPage.sdg_subtitle: HCW's programmes contribute directly to four global SDGs
impactPage.project_endara_beneficiaries: Pupils, students and young professionals in Bangui
impactPage.finance_mechanism: Our running costs are currently covered by the founder's personal contributions and by volunteers. As a result, 100% of your donation goes to our work on the ground.
impactPage.finance_commitment: As the association grows, we will publish the real breakdown of our spending every year, starting with the 2025 financial year.
impactPage.reports_coming_text: Our first public activity report, together with simplified accounts, will be published on this page in autumn 2026. Questions in the meantime: contact@h-cw.org.
impactPage.cta_text: Every euro counts. Your donation directly funds children's education in CAR.

about.title: About HCW
about.mission_text: HCW carries the initials of Hervé-Charles Wenezoui, a Doctor of Law and diplomat committed to the development of Central Africa. The association was founded in 2009 in his memory. We believe that openness to the world through knowledge and culture is an essential driver of development.
about.goal1_desc: Ensuring every child has equal access to education, regardless of their background.
about.goal2_desc: Strengthening the capacity of teachers in the Central African Republic.
about.goal3_desc: Supporting children and teenagers towards success with appropriate tools.
about.meta_description: Discover the story of HCW, an association founded in 2009 for education and culture in Central Africa. 9,000 students reached since 2009.
about.hero_subtitle: Since 2009, HCW has worked at the heart of Central Africa to give every child the chance to learn, grow, and flourish.
about.vision_text: A world where every Central African child has access to quality education, where culture is a vehicle for peace, and where solidarity builds bridges between peoples.
about.timeline_2009: HCW association founded in memory of Hervé-Charles Wenezoui
about.timeline_2013: Launch of first educational programmes in the Central African Republic
about.timeline_2018: Creation of the eNdara Challenge competition for youth innovation
about.timeline_2020: COVID-19 response: school kits distributed despite the crisis
about.team_subtitle: Dedicated people driven by the conviction that education changes the world.
about.team_photo_alt: HCW team and community partners in Bangui
about.team_photo_caption: Our team in Bangui, Central African Republic
about.team_volunteers: And many committed volunteers in France and CAR
about.partners_kaikelem_desc: Solidarity programme — widows and orphans CAR
about.values_education_desc: Ensuring equitable access to knowledge for every child, regardless of their background.
about.values_solidarity_desc: Acting together, with empathy, for vulnerable communities in Central Africa.
about.values_culture_desc: Valuing Central African cultural identities as a driver of development.
about.values_innovation_desc: Designing creative solutions adapted to on-the-ground realities.
about.cta_text: Volunteer, partner or donor — every commitment counts.

legal.meta_description: Legal notice for the HCW association — publisher, host, intellectual property and governing law.
legal.hero_subtitle: In accordance with French Law no. 2004-575 of 21 June 2004 on confidence in the digital economy (LCEN)
legal.legal_form_value: Non-profit association under French law of 1 July 1901
legal.director_text: The director of publication is Michel Wenezoui, founding President of the HCW association.
legal.host_details:
340 Pine Street Suite 701
San Francisco, CA 94104
United States
legal.ip_text_1: All content on this website (text, images, graphics, logo, icons, sounds, software…) is the exclusive property of the HCW association or its partners, and is protected by French and international intellectual property laws.
legal.ip_text_2: Any reproduction, distribution, modification, adaptation, retransmission or publication, even partial, of these various elements is strictly prohibited without the express written consent of HCW.
legal.liability_text_1: HCW strives to ensure the accuracy and currency of information published on this website. However, HCW cannot guarantee the accuracy, precision or completeness of information made available on this site.
legal.liability_text_2: Accordingly, HCW declines all liability for any inaccuracy, inexactitude or omission relating to information available on this site, and for any damage resulting from fraudulent intrusion by a third party that has led to a modification of information made available on this site.
legal.links_text: This website may contain hyperlinks to other websites. HCW has no control over such sites and is not responsible for their content. The creation of hyperlinks to the HCW website is subject to the prior consent of the publisher.
legal.law_text: This website is governed by French law. In the event of a dispute and failing amicable resolution, the courts of Oise (France) shall have sole jurisdiction.

privacy.meta_description: HCW privacy policy — data collected, GDPR rights, retention periods and hosting.
privacy.hero_subtitle: In compliance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) — EU 2016/679
privacy.data_intro: We only collect data strictly necessary for our activities:
privacy.purposes_body: Collected data is used exclusively to respond to your requests, send you our newsletter if you have consented, and improve the experience on our website. No data is sold or transferred to third parties for commercial purposes.
privacy.retention_stats_text: aggregated data, no personal data retained
privacy.retention_payment_text: retained by payment providers according to their legal obligations
privacy.rights_intro: Under GDPR, you have the following rights regarding your personal data:
privacy.rights_access_text: — obtain a copy of your data
privacy.rights_portability_text: — receive your data in a structured format
privacy.rights_contact: To exercise these rights, contact us at <emailLink>{email}</emailLink>. You may also lodge a complaint with the <cnil>CNIL</cnil> (French data protection authority).
privacy.cookies_p1: This website uses Plausible Analytics, a privacy-friendly audience analytics solution with no cookies, no collection of personally identifiable data, and no third-party data transfers. No cookie consent banner is required.
privacy.cookies_p2: No advertising tracking cookies or social media sharing cookies are used on this website.
privacy.hosting_p1: This website is hosted by Vercel Inc. (United States). Vercel operates under Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) approved by the European Commission, ensuring an adequate level of protection for data transfers outside the European Economic Area.
privacy.hosting_p2: Your data is never sold, rented or transferred to third parties.
privacy.changes_body: HCW reserves the right to modify this privacy policy at any time. Changes take effect upon publication on this page. We encourage you to check this page regularly.
